pr.title: Fix m2c by making `FighterVars_Size` a define
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Replaced the typed constant `FighterVars_Size` with the preprocessor macro `FIGHTERVARS_SIZE` across fighter variable struct padding and the main `Fighter_FighterVars` union, and updated m2ctx invocation to undefine `FIGHTERVARS_SIZE`. The stated goal was to fix m2c compatibility; evidence indicates this was a small header/tooling fix for how m2c/m2ctx handled the fighter vars size symbol.
what_changed: In `src/melee/ft/forward.h`, changed `static usize_t const FighterVars_Size ATTRIBUTE_USED = 0x100;` to `#define FIGHTERVARS_SIZE 0x100`.
Updated fighter-specific `*_FighterVars` padding arrays to use `FIGHTERVARS_SIZE` instead of `FighterVars_Size` in CLink, Captain, Link, Luigi, Mario, Mars, and Sandbag headers.
Updated the `union Fighter_FighterVars` backing byte array in `src/melee/ft/types.h` from `u8 _[FighterVars_Size];` to `u8 _[FIGHTERVARS_SIZE];`.
Added `-U FIGHTERVARS_SIZE` to the m2ctx preprocessor argument list in `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py`.
smart_moves: Kept the semantic size value unchanged at `0x100`, minimizing risk to fighter struct layout while changing only the representation of the constant.
Applied the rename consistently to all visible uses in fighter var padding and the central fighter vars union, avoiding mixed macro/constant usage.
Used an all-caps macro name, `FIGHTERVARS_SIZE`, matching normal C preprocessor constant style and making it visually distinct from typed symbols.
Adjusted m2ctx tooling alongside headers, indicating the fix was not just a source cleanup but targeted at generated m2c context behavior.
decomp_lessons: For constants used in array dimensions inside shared decomp headers, a preprocessor macro may be more compatible with m2c/m2ctx than a `static const` object, even if the value is compile-time known to the C compiler.
When changing a layout-size symbol, update every padding array and union backing array together so struct sizes remain synchronized.
Tooling-facing headers sometimes need different constraints than normal compilable source; a representation that is valid C may still interfere with m2c context generation.
Small header constants that exist only to define layout sizes should be reviewed for m2c parser/preprocessor compatibility before introducing typed globals.
naming_conventions: Macro constants use all caps with underscores: `FIGHTERVARS_SIZE`.
The old mixed-case typed constant form was `FighterVars_Size`; this PR replaced it rather than keeping both names.
Fighter-specific variable structs follow names like `ftMario_FighterVars`, `ftCaptain_FighterVars`, and `ftSandbag_FighterVars`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved byte-exact layout intent by leaving all padding arithmetic unchanged except for the symbol name, such as `FIGHTERVARS_SIZE - 0x18`, `FIGHTERVARS_SIZE - 0x20`, and `FIGHTERVARS_SIZE - 8`.
Centralized fighter vars storage remains a fixed-size `u8` array in `union Fighter_FighterVars`, now sized by the macro.
No assembly diff or explicit matching discussion was present in the PR slice; the matching relevance is inferred from m2c/m2ctx compatibility and struct layout preservation.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR context.
No reviewer rationale beyond the PR title and diff was available.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that reference `FIGHTERVARS_SIZE` to see whether the macro convention persisted.
Search for other `static const` size symbols used in array dimensions that may cause m2c or m2ctx issues.
Inspect m2ctx handling of `-U FIGHTERVARS_SIZE` to understand why undefining the macro is required for context generation.
Check whether other fighter character headers still use layout-size constants in a way inconsistent with m2c.
